input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's a complete setup plan for .NET MAUI on a clean macOS machine.

---

## 1. Install Xcode (iOS requirement)

- **Source:** Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com/xcode](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/)
- After install, run:
  ```
  xcode-select --install
  sudo xcodebuild -license accept
  ```
- **Version discovery:** MAUI docs specify minimum Xcode version per .NET release. Check [learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/get-started/installation](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/get-started/installation).

## 2. Install .NET SDK

- **Source:** [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) — get the latest **LTS** or **STS** release that supports MAUI.
- Alternatively, use the install script:
  ```
  curl -sSL https://dot.net/v1/dotnet-install.sh | bash /dev/stdin --channel 9.0
  ```
- Add to your shell profile:
  ```
  export DOTNET_ROOT=$HOME/.dotnet
  export PATH=$PATH:$DOTNET_ROOT:$DOTNET_ROOT/tools
  ```

## 3. Install the MAUI workload

```bash
dotnet workload install maui
```

- **Discover available workloads:** `dotnet workload search`
- **Check what's installed:** `dotnet workload list`

## 4. Install JDK (Android requirement)

The MAUI workload typically bundles the Android SDK/NDK, but you need a JDK:

- **Recommended:** Microsoft OpenJDK 17
- **Source:** [learn.microsoft.com/java/openjdk/download](https://learn.microsoft.com/java/openjdk/download)
- Or via Homebrew: `brew install --cask microsoft-openjdk@17`
- Set in your shell profile:
  ```
  export JAVA_HOME=$(/usr/libexec/java_home -v 17)
  ```

## 5. Android SDK setup

The `maui` workload installs Android SDK components automatically. Defaults go to:
```
~/Library/Developer/Xamarin/android-sdk-macosx
```

To manage manually or add emulator images:
```bash
dotnet android sdk list
dotnet android sdk install "system-images;android-35;google_apis;arm64-v8a"
```

Or install **Android Studio** ([developer.android.com/studio](https://developer.android.com/studio)) for the full SDK Manager GUI and emulator management.

## 6. (Optional but recommended) Install an IDE

| IDE | Source |
|---|---|
| **Visual Studio Code** + C# Dev Kit + .NET MAUI extension | [code.visualstudio.com](https://code.visualstudio.com) |
| **JetBrains Rider** | [jetbrains.com/rider](https://www.jetbrains.com/rider/) |

## 7. Verify everything

```bash
# .NET SDK
dotnet --info

# MAUI workload
dotnet workload list          # should show "maui"

# Xcode tools
xcode-select -p               # should print a path
xcrun simctl list devices      # lists iOS simulators

# JDK
java -version                  # should show 17.x

# Android
dotnet android sdk list        # shows installed Android components

# Create and build a test app
dotnet new maui -n TestApp
cd TestApp
dotnet build -f net9.0-ios
dotnet build -f net9.0-android
```

## 8. Version compatibility cheat sheet

| Check | Command / URL |
|---|---|
| Which .NET supports which MAUI | [learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/whats-new](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/whats-new/dotnet-9) |
| Required Xcode version | `dotnet workload install maui` prints warnings if mismatched |
| Android API levels | `dotnet android sdk list` |
| Workload update | `dotnet workload update` |

---

**TL;DR install order:** Xcode → .NET SDK → `dotnet workload install maui` → JDK 17 → verify with a test build. The workload handles most Android dependencies automatically.
